projects.direction: Design Lab grows the verified Lean 4 substrate itself: the Mathlib-adjacent pure mathematics the six libraries force into existence. Choquet capacitability, a constructive approximate minimax theorem from multiplicative weights, the sharp core of Pinsker's inequality, and the ergodic theory of finite Markov chains are all proved once, at the substrate layer, and every library above compiles against them.

projects.direction: Belief geometry as the representational medium a theory is made of. The original move is a role assignment — as general relativity assigned manifolds to spacetime, GEO1 assigns the belief simplex the role of native epistemic state space: a theory is first a region, trajectory, obstruction, and repair policy in that geometry, and equations or causal graphs are charts exported only after it stabilizes.
projects.note: Original to GEO1 (the substrate layer): the role assignment of belief geometry as native medium, partial theories as regions, the forward causal pairing, and conservative extension. The finite anchors are proved; the full do-calculus lift is open. Figure from the private shape-of-uncertainty repo — confirm public use.
projects.title: Belief geometry · GEO1

witnesses.claim: The centerpoint: the native object of theory formation is the geometry itself — belief state plus theory regions, allowed motions, diagnostics, and repair policies — and symbolic equations, causal graphs, and programmes are charts exported from it, not the thing being formed.

A partial theory is a region, not a sentence: a focal set is a set of mechanism profiles sharing the committed coordinates while leaving the rest open — ignorance carried as mass on a set, not forced into a probability over complete law names.

Forward causal pairing: a graph intervention induces a paired belief transport, and probability semantics is conserved exactly on the singleton-focal slice — the protected anchor. Lifting full graphical do-calculus beyond that slice is the open frontier.

The medium grows only conservatively: a vacuous frame refinement preserves old belief and plausibility and commutes with combination, but it also preserves hard conflict — so adding hidden coordinates demands explicit conservation assumptions, modelled by a forced representation cover.

projects.direction: Design Lab formalizes the statistical machinery a learning system runs on, and the statistics of auditing one. Stochastic approximation converges under martingale-difference noise and under i.i.d. sampling with Robbins-Monro step sizes, and the admission chain's multiple-testing control is itself a theorem: alpha-spending stays under budget and corrections can only make admission harder.

projects.direction: Log-loss audit is bounded below by the entropy of the data: the cross-entropy decomposition H(Q,P) = H(Q) + KL(Q||P) and Gibbs' inequality yield H(Q) <= H(Q,P), so an incompressible noise component pays only a finite audit-progress budget. The conditional version bounds compression progress on noisy components by the initial excess over the conditional entropy H(Y|X); the floor is derived rather than assumed, with the composition machine-checked end to end. This bears on the noisy-TV problem in curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation.
projects.title: Entropy Floors

witnesses.claim: Conditional entropy floor: progress on noisy components is bounded by the initial excess over H(Y|X).

theorem cond_entropy_floor_budget_crossEntropy (μ : FinitePMF X) (Q : X → FinitePMF H)
    (P : ι → X → FinitePMF H) [hP : ∀ θ, ∀ x, HasPositivePrior (P θ x)] (g : ℕ → ι) (T : ℕ) :
    cumCP (fun θ => condCrossEntropyFinitePMF μ Q (P θ)) g T
      ≤ condCrossEntropyFinitePMF μ Q (P (g 0)) - condEntropyFinitePMF μ Q
witnesses.name: cond_entropy_floor_budget_crossEntropy

projects.direction: The open neurosymbolic-routing programme: where in a transformer does continuous, certifiable neural computation hand off to discrete symbolic routing — and what does an agent pay in learnability and design budget for crossing that line? The learner model, read along attention’s temperature axis. (Its settled certificate + expressivity theorems live in §06 Knowledge.)

The cliff and design law are proved (the temperature instance of Gupta arXiv:2604.25028, Thm 4.1, at attention’s regularization boundary); what is open for the agent is using βStar and the config map as live controls — choosing routing temperature and architecture against a certified budget during self-improvement. witnesses.claim: The measurability cliff — the centerpiece: a strict descriptive-complexity drop located exactly at attention’s softmax/argmax boundary. Soft (neural) attention’s bad event is Borel — learnable and certifiable; the hard (symbolic) argmax mixture-of-experts cascade is analytic-but-non-Borel at every depth, yet stays learnable as null-measurable. Routing temperature is the neural↔symbolic dial.

witnesses.alt: Three-panel Borel-analytic separation: the singleton witness class over an analytic non-Borel set A; the proof chain bad-event → Suslin projection → analytic → Choquet capacity → null-measurable, against the failed assume-Borel path; and the strict gap WellBehavedVC HOLDS vs KrappWirth FAILS.
witnesses.claim: The Borel-analytic separation — the standout: the first concrete concept-class witness inhabiting the strict gap between an analytic-non-Borel bad event and a Borel one. WellBehavedVC holds (the bad event is null-measurable) while Krapp–Wirth fails (it is not Borel). The full counterexample proof is below.

witnesses.code:
-- The entire Borel-analytic counterexample (exists_measTarget_separation)

WITNESS.   Let A ⊆ ℝ be analytic, non-Borel (exists under
  standard set theory). Define the singleton class
      singletonClassOn(A) = {zeroConcept} ∪ {singletonConcept a | a ∈ A}
  where singletonConcept a returns true only at x = a.

STEP 1  (every hypothesis is Borel).
  zeroConcept is constant; each singletonConcept a is piecewise
  constant on the measurable singleton {a}.  So every hypothesis in
  the class is Borel-measurable.

STEP 2  (reduce to a planar witness).
  The uniform-convergence bad event — ghost-sample pairs on which
  some hypothesis has empirical gap ≥ ε/2 — reduces, by a preimage
  calculation, to the planar witness event W ⊆ ℝ².

STEP 3  (W is analytic ⇒ null-measurable).
  W = { (training, ghost) : ∃ a ∈ A. training misses a ∧ ghost hits a }.
  W is an existential projection of a Borel set, hence analytic (Σ¹₁)
  by Suslin's theorem (1917).  Analytic sets are universally
  measurable by Choquet capacitability (1954; Kechris 30.13, formalized
  here) — so W is NullMeasurableSet.            ⊢ WellBehavedVCMeasTarget

STEP 4  (W is not Borel).
  Suppose W were Borel.  Then its preimage under the constant section
      x ↦ (a₀, x)
  would be Borel.  But that preimage is A itself — not Borel. ⇒⇐
                                                 ⊬ KrappWirthWellBehaved

CONCLUSION.
  WellBehavedVCMeasTarget holds while KrappWirthWellBehaved fails:
  the gap between NullMeasurableSet and MeasurableSet is inhabited by
  a concrete concept class.                      ∎  (axioms: propext,
                                                    Classical.choice, Quot.sound)

projects.direction: Splitting clustered trajectory data at the frame level instead of the episode level leaves a Bayes L2 risk gap equal to an explained-variance functional of the two nested sigma-algebras; the gap does not vanish and cannot be closed by adding data within episodes. In the one-way random-effects model the variance of the grand mean decomposes exactly so that the effective sample size is the group count G, not the frame count G*m, and the Hoeffding tail of the between-group component is identical across models that differ only in frame count. A finite construction shows the method-of-moments variance-component estimate is negative with probability at least (1-2p)^G even though the true variance is 2p > 0.
projects.title: Split Leakage · Effective Sample Size

witnesses.claim: The frame-vs-episode leakage gap equals an intrinsic explained-variance functional of the two sigma-algebras.
witnesses.code:
theorem bayesRisk_gap [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (hm₁₂ : m₁ ≤ m₂) (hm₂ : m₂ ≤ m₀) (hX : MemLp X 2 μ) :
    bayesRisk m₁ X μ - bayesRisk m₂ X μ = μ[Var[μ[X | m₂]; μ | m₁]]

witnesses.claim: The episode-frame risk gap does not vanish and is not closeable by adding data within episodes.

    (hX : MemLp X 2 μ) (hXm : StronglyMeasurable X) :
    bayesRisk (⊥ : MeasurableSpace Ω) X μ - bayesRisk m₀ X μ = variance X μ

witnesses.claim: Exact one-way random-effects variance decomposition: effective sample size is the group count G, not the frame count G*m.
witnesses.code:
theorem variance_grandMean_eq :
    variance M.grandMean M.μ = M.σb ^ 2 / M.G + M.σw ^ 2 / (M.G * M.m)

witnesses.claim: The Hoeffding tail of the between-group component is identical across models that differ only in frame count m.
A finite probability space where the variance-component estimate is negative with probability at least (1-2p)^G while the true variance is 2p > 0.
witnesses.code:
theorem negative_r2_prob_floor {p : ℝ} (hp0 : 0 < p) (hp1 : p < 1 / 2)
    {w : ℝ} (hw : 0 < w) :
    0 < variance effectVal (singlePMF hp0.le hp1.le).toMeasure ∧
      ENNReal.ofReal ((1 - 2 * p) ^ G)
        ≤ (jointPMF G hp0.le hp1.le).toMeasure {c | sigmaHatB w c < 0} ∧
      0 < (1 - 2 * p) ^ G

projects.direction: Softmax routing keeps the uniform-convergence bad events of attention Borel, while argmax routing produces bad events that are analytic but not Borel: a strict descriptive-complexity drop located at the softmax/argmax boundary, with routing temperature acting as the toggle at every depth. The dichotomy is governed by the parameter space: sigma-compact spaces admit no such pathology, while Polish non-sigma-compact spaces carry a binary attention router whose bad event is analytic and non-Borel. The same measurability condition is the precondition on which the positive executed certificates stand.
projects.note: The non-Borel constructions take the classical existence of an analytic non-Borel set of reals as an explicit hypothesis.
projects.title: The Measurability Cliff

projects.direction: True mutual information is invariant under coordinatewise measurable reparametrization, but the KSG k-nearest-neighbor estimator is not: its radius core scales linearly under uniform similarity transformations. Per-axis standardization restores invariance of the radius statistic under the full diagonal-rescaling group. These estimator-geometry failure modes, together with training non-stationarity, are witnessed on a real JEPA world model by tracking effective rank, KSG mutual-information estimates, validation prediction loss, and linear probe R^2 across training at 64px and 128px resolution on dm_control Reacher.

projects.direction: Symmetrization arguments for PAC learning need the bad event to be measurable, and the Borel condition can be weakened to null-measurability. For a Borel-parameterized concept class the ghost-gap witness set is Borel, its projection over parameters is analytic by Suslin's projection theorem, and analytic sets are null-measurable under finite Borel measures on Polish spaces by Choquet capacitability. Every Borel-parameterized concept class therefore satisfies the null-measurable event-level condition the symmetrization proof requires.
projects.note: These statements are machine-checked in Lean 4 with no open proof obligations. They come from a ten-problem automated proof-search benchmark on research-level mathematics whose outcomes were four accepted, four partial, and two rejected.
projects.title: Learner Measurability · Borel-Analytic Bridge

witnesses.claim: The witness set of parameter and sample pairs with gap at least ε/2 is Borel when the evaluator and target are measurable.

theorem paramWitnessSet_measurable
    {X : Type u} [MeasurableSpace X]

    (e : Θ → Concept X Bool)

    (c : Concept X Bool) (hc : Measurable c)
    (m : ℕ) (ε : ℝ) :
    MeasurableSet (paramWitnessSet e c m ε)

witnesses.claim: The bad event, the existential projection of the witness set over parameters, is analytic by Suslin's projection theorem.

    MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet (paramBadEvent e c m ε)

projects.direction: The null-measurable condition is strictly separated from the Borel supremum condition: any analytic non-Borel subset of the reals yields a concept class that satisfies the first and fails the second. The weakened condition also carries a closure algebra under concept-class constructors, preserved by piecewise patching and by fiber-product amalgamation of concept families.
projects.note: The separation theorem is parametric: it converts any analytic non-Borel subset of the reals into a separating concept class.

projects.direction: Online learnability of a concept class is equivalent to finiteness of its Littlestone dimension, and for nonempty classes the optimal mistake bound equals the Littlestone dimension exactly. In the identification-in-the-limit setting, EX-learnability is characterized by a finite mind-change count, and Gold's theorem rules out EX-identification of any class containing all finite sets together with an infinite set; the proof runs through a fully explicit adversarial locking sequence.

projects.direction: The McAllester PAC-Bayes bound is proved from first principles via Hoeffding's inequality and Jensen's inequality: for a finite hypothesis class, with probability at least 1 - delta over m independent samples, the Gibbs error of every posterior Q is at most its empirical Gibbs error plus sqrt((H_cross(Q, P) + log(1/delta)) / (2m)). The Efron-Stein inequality bounds the variance of a function of n independent random variables in a product probability space by the sum of coordinate-wise conditional variance terms.
projects.note: The PAC-Bayes statement is proved for a finite hypothesis class with an everywhere-positive prior.
projects.title: Concentration and Generalization

witnesses.claim: McAllester bound for a finite hypothesis class: Gibbs error at most empirical Gibbs error plus sqrt((H_cross(Q, P) + log(1/delta)) / (2m)).
witnesses.code:
theorem pac_bayes_finite {X : Type u} [MeasurableSpace X]
    {H : Type*} [Fintype H] [Nonempty H]
    (D : MeasureTheory.Measure X) [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure D]
    (c : Concept X Bool) (hc_meas : Measurable c)
    (hs : H → Concept X Bool) (hhs_meas : ∀ h, Measurable (hs h))
    (P : FinitePMF H) (hP_pos : ∀ h, 0 < P.prob h)
    (m : ℕ) (hm : 0 < m) (δ : ℝ) (hδ : 0 < δ) (hδ1 : δ ≤ 1) :
    MeasureTheory.Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin m => D)
      { S : Fin m → X |
        ∀ (Q : FinitePMF H),
          gibbsError Q hs c D ≤
            gibbsEmpError Q hs c S +
            Real.sqrt ((crossEntropyFinitePMF Q P + Real.log (1 / δ)) / (2 * ↑m)) }
      ≥ ENNReal.ofReal (1 - δ) :=

witnesses.claim: Efron-Stein: the variance of a function of n independent variables is bounded by summed conditional variance terms.
witnesses.code:
theorem efronStein (f : (Fin n → Ω) → ℝ) (hf : MemLp f 2 μˢ) :
    variance f μˢ ≤ ∑ i : Fin n, ∫ x, (f x - condExpExceptCoord (μs := μs) i f x)^2 ∂μˢ :=

projects.direction: Every analytic subset of a Polish space is capacitable: a Choquet capacity evaluated on an analytic set equals the supremum of its values over compact subsets. Analytic sets are also null-measurable with respect to every finite Borel measure on a Polish space, a foundational bridge for RKHS kernel learning theory.
projects.note: The capacitability proof mechanizes the Baire-space diagonal construction directly in dependent type theory; the statement follows Kechris, Classical Descriptive Set Theory, Theorem 30.13.
projects.title: Analytic Sets · Capacitability

    (hcap : MeasureTheory.IsChoquetCapacity cap)
    {s : Set α} (hs : MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet s) :
    cap s = ⨆ (K : Set α), ⨆ (_ : IsCompact K), ⨆ (_ : K ⊆ s), cap K

projects.direction: Pinsker's inequality holds with the sharp constant: the total variation distance between probability measures is at most the square root of half the KL divergence. On the kernel side, the mean embedding reproduces integrals, squared MMD vanishes exactly when two measures coincide, and HSIC vanishes exactly when a joint law equals the product of its marginals, in each case under a characteristic kernel.
projects.note: The binary case is proved by an explicit derivative-based monotonicity argument for the Pinsker residual.
projects.title: Distribution Discrepancies

    (P Q : Measure α) [IsProbabilityMeasure P] [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    (hac : P.AbsolutelyContinuous Q) (h_int : Integrable (llr P Q) P) :
    tvDistReal P Q ≤ Real.sqrt (klDivReal P Q / 2)

    2 * (p - q)^2 ≤ klBin p q

    (hcont : Continuous (fun x => kerFun H x (1 : ℝ)))
    (f : H) :
    @inner ℝ H _ (kernelMeanEmbedding (H := H) P) f = ∫ x, f x ∂P

projects.direction: Multiplicative weights reaches an approximate minimax solution: O(log N / ε²) rounds produce a mixed strategy with payoff at least v - ε against every pure column. The Sauer-Shelah lemma bounds the size of a set family with bounded VC dimension, and Assouad's dual bound gives VC dimension at most 2^{d+1} - 1 for the transpose when the original matrix has VC dimension at most d. A Chebyshev argument amplifies k ≥ 9/δ independent events, each of probability at least 2/3, into a majority holding with probability at least 1 - δ.
projects.note: The Sauer-Shelah proof is routed through Mathlib's shatterer API; the majority amplification lemma is a standard boosting primitive.
projects.title: Minimax and VC Bounds

witnesses.claim: MWU approximate minimax: O(log N / ε²) rounds yield a mixed strategy achieving payoff at least v - ε against all pure columns.
witnesses.code:
theorem mwu_approx_minimax
    {R C : Type*} [Fintype R] [Fintype C] [Nonempty R] [Nonempty C]
    [DecidableEq R] [DecidableEq C]
    (M : R → C → Bool) (v ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε)
    (hrow : ∀ q : FintypePMF C, ∃ r : R,
      v ≤ ∑ c, q.prob c * (if M r c then (1 : ℝ) else 0)) :
    ∃ p : FintypePMF R, ∀ c : C, v - ε ≤ boolGamePayoff M p c

witnesses.claim: Sauer-Shelah bound on the size of a set family with bounded VC dimension.
witnesses.code:
theorem card_toFinsetFamily_le {m n : ℕ} (M : BinaryMatrix m n)
    {d : ℕ} (hd : M.toFinsetFamily.vcDim ≤ d) :
    M.toFinsetFamily.card ≤ ∑ k ∈ Finset.Iic d, n.choose k

witnesses.claim: Assouad's dual VC bound: VCDim(Mᵀ) ≤ 2^{d+1} - 1 when VCDim(M) ≤ d.
witnesses.code:

    {d : ℕ} (hd : M.vcDim ≤ d) :
    M.transpose.vcDim ≤ 2 ^ (d + 1) - 1

witnesses.claim: Majority amplification: k ≥ 9/δ independent events of probability at least 2/3 give a majority with probability at least 1 - δ.
witnesses.code:
lemma ProbabilityTheory.chebyshev_majority_bound
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω}
    [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {k : ℕ} {δ : ℝ} (h_delta_pos : 0 < δ)
    (hk : (9 : ℝ) / δ ≤ k)
    (events : Fin k → Set Ω)
    (hevents_meas : ∀ j, MeasurableSet (events j))
    (hindep : ProbabilityTheory.iIndepSet (fun j => events j) μ)
    (hprob : ∀ j, μ (events j) ≥ ENNReal.ofReal (2/3)) :
    μ {ω | k < 2 * (Finset.univ.filter (fun j => ω ∈ events j)).card} ≥
      ENNReal.ofReal (1 - δ)

projects.direction: The five-way fundamental theorem of statistical learning is machine-checked in full, with PAC learnability, finite VC dimension, and finite sample compression among the equivalent characterizations, and no open proof obligations remain. The compression characterization runs through a constructive approximate minimax theorem obtained from multiplicative weights in place of Sion's exact minimax. Machine-verified alongside it are sharp combinatorial results, the exact VC dimension of halfspaces in R^n and Assouad's dual VC bound for binary matrices, and a McAllester PAC-Bayes bound that supplies a frequentist to Bayesian bridge.
projects.title: Statistical Learning · Fundamental Theorem

witnesses.claim: The five-way fundamental theorem of statistical learning, complete and sorry-free in Lean 4.
witnesses.code:
theorem fundamental_theorem (X : Type u) [MeasurableSpace X]
    [MeasurableSingletonClass X]
    (C : ConceptClass X Bool)
    [MeasurableConceptClass X C] :
    (PACLearnable X C ↔ VCDim X C < ⊤) ∧
    ((VCDim X C < ⊤) ↔ ∃ (k : ℕ) (cs : CompressionSchemeWithInfo0 X Bool C), cs.size = k) ∧ ...

theorem mwu_approx_minimax
    {R C : Type*} [Fintype R] [Fintype C] [Nonempty R] [Nonempty C]
    [DecidableEq R] [DecidableEq C]
    (M : R → C → Bool) (v ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε)
    (hrow : ∀ q : FinitePMF C, ∃ r : R,
      v ≤ ∑ c, q.prob c * (if M r c then (1 : ℝ) else 0)) :
    ∃ p : FinitePMF R, ∀ c : C, v - ε ≤ boolGamePayoff M p c

witnesses.claim: The McAllester PAC-Bayes generalization bound, a frequentist to Bayesian bridge in Lean 4.
witnesses.code:
theorem pac_bayes_finite {X : Type u} [MeasurableSpace X]
    {H : Type*} [Fintype H] [Nonempty H]
    (D : MeasureTheory.Measure X) [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure D]
    (c : Concept X Bool) (hc_meas : Measurable c)
    (hs : H → Concept X Bool) (hhs_meas : ∀ h, Measurable (hs h))
    (P : FinitePMF H) (hP_pos : ∀ h, 0 < P.prob h)
    (m : ℕ) (hm : 0 < m) (δ : ℝ) (hδ : 0 < δ) (hδ1 : δ ≤ 1) :
    MeasureTheory.Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin m => D)
      { S : Fin m → X | ∀ (Q : FinitePMF H),
          gibbsError Q hs c D ≤ gibbsEmpError Q hs c S +
            Real.sqrt ((crossEntropyFinitePMF Q P + Real.log (1 / δ)) / (2 * ↑m)) }
      ≥ ENNReal.ofReal (1 - δ)

projects.direction: PAC learning has a measurability core: uniform convergence arguments need the bad events to be regular enough to measure. Choquet's capacitability theorem yields that analytic sets are null-measurable, bridging capacity theory to learning, and a strict separation witness shows the null-measurable regularity condition for PAC bad events is distinct from the Borel one. The condition is preserved under amalgamation of concept classes over standard Borel parameter spaces, and a concrete version space learner is verified to pass the measurability gate.
projects.note: The Borel versus null-measurable separation is proved relative to an explicit hypothesis: the existence of an analytic set of reals that is not measurable.
projects.title: Learner Measurability

witnesses.claim: Choquet's capacitability theorem (Kechris 30.13): capacities of analytic sets in Polish spaces are approximated from within by compact sets.
witnesses.code:
theorem MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet.cap_eq_iSup_isCompact
    {α : Type*}
    [TopologicalSpace α] [MeasurableSpace α] [BorelSpace α] [PolishSpace α]
    {cap : Set α → ENNReal}
    (hcap : MeasureTheory.IsChoquetCapacity cap)
    {s : Set α} (hs : MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet s) :
    cap s = ⨆ (K : Set α), ⨆ (_ : IsCompact K), ⨆ (_ : K ⊆ s), cap K

witnesses.claim: Analytic sets are null-measurable: the bridge from Choquet capacity to PAC learning measurability.
witnesses.code:
theorem analyticSet_nullMeasurableSet
    {α : Type*}
    [TopologicalSpace α] [MeasurableSpace α] [BorelSpace α] [PolishSpace α]
    {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α} [MeasureTheory.IsFiniteMeasure μ]
    {s : Set α} (hs : MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet s) :
    MeasureTheory.NullMeasurableSet s μ

witnesses.claim: Null-measurable and Borel regularity conditions for PAC bad events are strictly separated, given an analytic non-measurable set of reals.
witnesses.code:
theorem exists_measTarget_separation
    (hex : ∃ A : Set ℝ, MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet A ∧ ¬ MeasurableSet A) :
    KrappWirthSeparationMeasTarget

witnesses.claim: Amalgamation of concept classes over standard Borel parameter spaces preserves null-measurability.
witnesses.code:
theorem amalgClass_wellBehaved
    {X : Type u}
    [MeasurableSpace X] [TopologicalSpace X] [PolishSpace X] [BorelSpace X]
    {Θ₁ Θ₂ S : Type*}
    [MeasurableSpace Θ₁] [StandardBorelSpace Θ₁]
    [MeasurableSpace Θ₂] [StandardBorelSpace Θ₂]
    [MeasurableSpace S] [StandardBorelSpace S]
    (π₁ : Θ₁ → S) (π₂ : Θ₂ → S)
    (hπ₁ : Measurable π₁) (hπ₂ : Measurable π₂)
    (merge : Θ₁ × Θ₂ → Concept X Bool)
    (hmerge : Measurable (fun p : (Θ₁ × Θ₂) × X => merge p.1 p.2)) :
    WellBehavedVCMeasTarget X (amalgClass π₁ π₂ merge)

projects.direction: Q-learning converges almost surely to the optimal Q-function, and linear TD to its fixed point, under Markovian sampling with inverse-polynomial step sizes, all machine-checked. The proofs share a single Lyapunov-based recursive error bound and bottom out in the Robbins-Siegmund almost-supermartingale convergence theorem. For Q-learning the Lyapunov candidate is the squared l_p norm, whose smoothness with constant p - 1 is proved separately.
projects.note: The Markovian-sampling theorems are stated for inverse-polynomial step-size schedules with exponent in the interval (2/3, 1), as in the theorem hypotheses.
projects.title: Stochastic Approximation · TD and Q-Learning

witnesses.claim: Q-learning converges almost surely to the optimal Q-function under Markovian samples.
witnesses.code:
theorem ae_tendsto_of_QLearning_markov
  {ν : ℝ} (hν : ν ∈ Set.Ioo (2 / 3) 1)
  (hq : QLearningIterates (spec := spec) (q := q))
  (hα : spec.α = fun n : ℕ => inv_poly ν 2 n) :
  ∀ᵐ ω ∂ spec.MRP.markov_samples,
    Tendsto (fun n => q n ω) atTop (𝓝 spec.optimal_q)

  (hw : LinearTDIterates (spec := spec) (w := w))
  (hα : spec.α = fun n : ℕ => inv_poly ν 2 n) :
  ∀ᵐ ω ∂ spec.markov_samples,
    Tendsto (fun n => w n ω) atTop (𝓝 spec.td_fixed_point)

witnesses.claim: The Robbins-Siegmund almost-supermartingale theorem, the foundation of the stochastic approximation proofs.
witnesses.code:
theorem ae_tendsto_zero_of_almost_supermartingale
    (hfnonneg : ∀ n, 0 ≤ᵐ[μ] f n)
    {T : ℕ → ℝ} (hTpos : ∀ n, 0 < T n)
    (hAlmostSupermartingale :
      ∃ C ≥ 0, ∀ n, μ[f (n + 1) | ℱ n] ≤ᵐ[μ] (fun ω => (1 - T n) * f n ω + C * T n ^ 2)) :
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, Tendsto (fun n => f n ω) atTop (𝓝 0)

witnesses.claim: A Lyapunov-based recursive error bound shared by all the convergence proofs.
witnesses.code:
theorem fundamental_inequality
  {x : ℕ → Ω → E d}
  (hx : Iterates x x₀ f e₁ e₂ α)
  ...
  [LyapunovFunction φ φ' f] :
  ∃ B₁ B₂, 0 < B₁ ∧ 0 ≤ B₂ ∧ ∃ n₀, ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, ∀ n, n ≥ n₀ →
    φ (x (n + 1) ω - z) ≤ (1 - B₁ * α n) * φ (x n ω - z) +
      ⟪φ' (x n ω - z), e₁ (n + 1) ω⟫ + B₂ * (α n) ^ 2

witnesses.claim: Smoothness of the squared l_p norm with constant p - 1, the Lyapunov candidate for Q-learning.
witnesses.code:
theorem smooth_half_sq_Lp (hp : 2 ≤ p) :
  ∀ (x y : LpSpace p d),
    half_sq_Lp y ≤ half_sq_Lp x + ⟪toL2 (half_sq_Lp' x), toL2 (y - x)⟫ + (p - 1) * half_sq_Lp (y - x)

projects.direction: Machine-verified zero conditions for standard discrepancy measures: MMD vanishes exactly when two distributions are equal, provided the kernel is characteristic; HSIC vanishes exactly when the joint distribution factorizes into its marginals, again under a characteristic-kernel hypothesis on the product space; and mutual information is zero if and only if independence holds, under an absolute continuity hypothesis. Pinsker's inequality with its sharp constant bounds total variation by the square root of half the KL divergence, and the reproducing property of the kernel mean embedding grounds the RKHS results.
projects.note: The mutual information characterization carries explicit absolute continuity and finite KL divergence hypotheses, stated as part of the theorem.
projects.title: Discrepancies and Independence

witnesses.claim: Pinsker's inequality with sharp constant, fully proved: total variation is at most the square root of half the KL divergence.
witnesses.code:
theorem pinsker_proof
    (P Q : Measure α) [IsProbabilityMeasure P] [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    (hac : P.AbsolutelyContinuous Q) (h_int : Integrable (llr P Q) P) :
    tvDistReal P Q ≤ Real.sqrt (klDivReal P Q / 2)

witnesses.claim: For a characteristic kernel, MMD vanishes exactly when the two distributions are equal.
witnesses.code:
theorem mmdSq_zero_iff
    (hchar : IsCharacteristic (H := H) (X := X))
    (P Q : Measure X) :
    mmdSq (H := H) P Q = 0 ↔ P = Q

witnesses.claim: For a characteristic kernel on the product space, HSIC vanishes exactly when the joint distribution is the product of its marginals.
witnesses.code:
theorem hsicDef_zero_iff_independent
    (hchar : IsCharacteristic (H := H) (X := X × Y))
    (P : Measure (X × Y)) :
    hsicDef (H := H) P = 0 ↔ P = (P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)

witnesses.claim: Mutual information equals zero if and only if the variables are independent, under an absolute continuity hypothesis.
witnesses.code:
theorem mutualInformationReal_eq_zero_iff_product {α β : Type*}
    [MeasurableSpace α] [MeasurableSpace β]
    (P : Measure (α × β)) [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    (hac : P.AbsolutelyContinuous ((P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)))
    (hfin : InformationTheory.klDiv P ((P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)) ≠ ⊤) :
    mutualInformationReal P = 0 ↔ P = (P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)

witnesses.claim: The reproducing property of the kernel mean embedding: inner products against the embedding compute expectations.
witnesses.code:
theorem kernelMeanEmbedding_reproducing
    (P : Measure X) [IsFiniteMeasure P]
    (hcont : Continuous (fun x => kerFun H x (1 : ℝ)))
    (f : H) :
    @inner ℝ H _ (kernelMeanEmbedding (H := H) P) f = ∫ x, f x ∂P

projects.direction: TorchLean is a typed, executable neural network framework whose robustness certificates are checkable in Lean: typed tensors and a shared graph IR feed verified reverse-mode autograd and IEEE-754 semantics, and bound propagation with affine and ReLU relaxations carries a perturbation set through the network to certified output bounds. Supporting analysis is formalized alongside: ReLU is 1-Lipschitz in L2 for tensors of any shape, a per-step squared-norm contraction bound holds for gradient steps on strongly monotone Lipschitz operators, and one-dimensional ridge regression has an explicit uniform stability constant.
projects.title: Verified Neural Networks · TorchLean

witnesses.claim: ReLU is 1-Lipschitz in L2 for any tensor shape, proved by structural induction on the shape type.
witnesses.code:
theorem relu_lipschitz_general {s : Shape} (x y : Tensor ℝ s) :
  tensorL2Dist (reluSpec x) (reluSpec y) ≤ tensorL2Dist x y

witnesses.claim: A per-step squared-norm contraction bound for gradient steps on strongly monotone Lipschitz operators.
witnesses.code:
theorem step_norm_sq_le (η μ : ℝ) (hη : 0 ≤ η) {L : NNReal} (g : E → E)
    (hmono : StrongMonotone μ g) (hlip : LipschitzWith L g)
    (x y : E) :
    ‖step η g x - step η g y‖ ^ 2 ≤ (1 - 2 * η * μ + (η ^ 2) * (L : ℝ) ^ 2) * ‖x - y‖ ^ 2

witnesses.claim: Uniform stability of one-dimensional ridge regression with an explicit stability constant.
witnesses.code:
theorem ridgeFit1D_sqLoss_uniformStableReplace (hlam : 0 < lam) :
    UniformStableReplace (Z := BoundedExample X Y) (H := ℝ)
      (A := fun S => ridgeFit1D (n := n) (X := X) (Y := Y) lam S)
      (ℓ := fun w z => sqLoss (X := X) (Y := Y) w z)
      (β := 4 * X ^ 2 * Y ^ 2 * (lam + X ^ 2) ^ 2 / (lam ^ 3 * N (n := n)))
